nativeRange.habitat: Seaward reefs and shallow semi-exposed hard-coral and rock reef with rich algae growth; depth range roughly 1–25 m, typically shallower than 10 m.

diet.notes: Primarily grazes microalgae and film algae from live rock and substrate. Has been documented accepting vegetable-based supplements such as dried spirulina, nori, and commercial herbivore preparations. Generally advised to house in a mature, well-established aquarium with ample natural algae growth.

avoidWith: other Crossosalarias or similar-shaped combtooth blennies
conspecifics unless introduced as a mated pair in a larger system

summary: A small, mottled herbivorous blenny from Western Pacific reefs, often seen perching on and hopping between rocks while grazing algae. Generally considered peaceful and reef-safe; best kept singly.
careNotes:
Generally advised to introduce into a mature reef tank (6+ months) with plentiful microalgae on rockwork. Has been known to display aggression toward other blennies and fish with similar body shapes; typically recommended to keep singly unless a proven mated pair is kept in a larger system. Generally considered reef-safe. Like most blennies, has been known to jump, so a tightly fitted lid is advised.

Temperature cross-source disagreement: LiveAquaria (aquarium recommendation) suggests 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C); FishBase wild habitat data records preferred range of 24.6–29.3°C (mean 28.5°C). Averaged min: 23.0°C, max: 27.5°C. No needs_review triggered as the spread is not 2×, but note that standard reef temperatures (25–26°C) fall comfortably within both ranges.

Tank size: LiveAquaria recommends 30 US gallons (~114 L); no competing recommendation found in secondaries. Rounded up to 120 L (nearest nice value above 114 L). FishBase common name for this species is 'Tripplespot Blenny'; 'One Spot Blenny' is the common aquarium trade name.
breedingNotes: Oviparous with distinct pairing behavior. Eggs are demersal and adhesive, attached to substrate via a filamentous adhesive pad. No captive breeding reports were found in the sources consulted; breeding in home aquariums has not been well documented.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary husbandry reference: adult size 4 in (~10 cm), temp 72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12, min tank 30 gal, easy care level, herbivore, peaceful, reef-compatible.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Taxonomy and wild biology cross-check: Family Blenniidae, Order Blenniiformes, max 10.0 cm TL, Western Pacific (east to Tonga) including Australia, Indonesia, Japan, Philippines, Papua New Guinea, Palau, Fiji, Samoa, Solomon Islands, New Caledonia, Tonga, Vietnam, Ryukyu Islands. Depth 1–25 m. Wild preferred temperature 24.6–29.3°C (mean 28.5°C). Oviparous with demersal adhesive eggs. Designated secondary (Reef2Reef wiki) returned HTTP 403; FishBase used as secondary per slice fallback order.

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous with demersal adhesive eggs; no reliable captive breeding or larval rearing accounts found in sources consulted.
